title: selinum opacum
intro: Selinum opacum, also known as wild celery or smallage, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its hollow stems and flat-topped clusters of small yellowish flowers. The medicinal properties of Selinum opacum include its ability to act as a diuretic, reducing water retention and alleviating conditions such as bloating and edema. The plant is also rich in vitamins and minerals, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Selinum opacum is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
